title: cjkcodecs: shared iso2022 MultibyteIncrementalDecoder reset() races getstate() on the codec state field (iso2022_decode_reset vs _PyLong_FromByteArray)
description: An iso2022 MultibyteIncrementalDecoder keeps its ISO-2022 designation state in a plain inline 8-byte field self->state.c. reset() rewrites it via the decreset callback (iso2022_decode_reset: STATE_SETG0 -> 1-byte write of self->state.c[0], _codecs_iso2022.c:314) while getstate() reads the whole field via _PyLong_FromByteArray(self->state.c, ...) (byte read at longobject.c:1069). No lock / critical section, so a decoder shared across threads is a data race on self->state.c. Same underlying unsynchronized-decoder-state bug as TSAN-0001 (different field/face, same fleet vehicle).
sites: Modules/cjkcodecs/_codecs_iso2022.c iso2022_decode_reset:314 (write self->state.c[0] = CHARSET_ASCII via STATE_SETG0, 1-byte; reached from reset_impl:1344 decreset callback)
Objects/longobject.c _PyLong_FromByteArray:1069 (read *p, a 1-byte read of self->state.c[i]; reached from getstate_impl:1266 _PyLong_FromByteArray(self->state.c, 8, ...))
race_kind: unsynchronized mutable object state (no critical section / GIL-era stateful C object)
shared_state: MultibyteIncrementalDecoderObject.state (MultibyteCodec_State: unsigned char c[8], inline in _MultibyteStatefulCodec_HEAD, multibytecodec.h); reported race is on state.c[0], the ISO-2022 G0 charset-designation byte. Same object also carries the unguarded pending[8]/pendingsize fields (TSAN-0001).
severity: low-medium (reported reset/getstate pairing on state.c[0] is value-benign & crash-free -- a 1-byte store of a constant; the same unguarded object also has decode()/setstate() mutating state+pending+pendingsize as a unit, which the wider family can tear)
repro_reliability: deterministic-ish (exit 66, races within ~1-2s); with 1 writer (reset) + 5 readers (getstate) ~12/15 runs catch the exact seeded pair (iso2022_decode_reset:314 write vs _PyLong_FromByteArray:1069 read), the rest catch adjacent faces of the same object-state race (reset/reset write-write on state.c[0], or the pendingsize face from TSAN-0001)
suggested_fix: add per-object critical sections to the incremental encoder/decoder + stream reader/writer methods (Argument Clinic @critical_section on decode/getstate/setstate/reset, or Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION(self)); a lone atomic on any single field is insufficient because state+pending+pendingsize form one logical state accessed as a unit. Same fix as TSAN-0001, covers both.
duplicate_of: TSAN-0001 (same unsynchronized MultibyteIncrementalDecoder state; TSAN-0001 = pendingsize face, TSAN-0004 = state.c face; same fleet vehicle; tsan_dedup split them because the racing function pairs differ)
notes: multibytecodec.c has ZERO Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION and no @critical_section clinic annotations; the clinic wrappers (getstate:420, reset:466) are trivial pass-throughs with no locking. state.c[0] holds the ISO-2022 G0 charset designation. This is NOT an independent bug from TSAN-0001 -- it is a second racing field of the same shared decoder, from the same vehicle, resolved by the same whole-family critical-section fix. If filed upstream, fold TSAN-0001 + TSAN-0004 into one report on the multibytecodec.c incremental/stream CJK codec family.
assessment: real, but DUPLICATE of TSAN-0001 (same MultibyteIncrementalDecoder, state.c face vs pendingsize face) — fold into one report

related_issues.note: same file as TSAN-0001; Modules/cjkcodecs/multibytecodec.c UNCHECKED on gh-116738.

fleet02_notes: fleet-02: HZ/gb codec (_codecs_cn.c) reset-vs-read -- same cjkcodecs incremental-decoder-state class as iso2022, another codec face
